scenario_title: Delete_rrset of a non-existent type after name existence is established
test_rationale: This test isolates the underspecified corner where an UPDATE first proves a name exists via a prerequisite and then issues delete_rrset for a type that is not present at that name. The zone starts with only an A RRset at n.campus.edu., so TXT is definitely absent. Because the operation is semantically empty, servers differ in whether they return NOERROR as a no-op or NXRRSET as an error, while the ensuing queries confirm that the zone content itself remains unchanged.

group_1.behavior: Treats delete_rrset for a non-existent RRset as a harmless no-op once the name prerequisite is satisfied.
group_1.post_state: No change; n.campus.edu. retains only its A RRset and remains TXT-absent.

group_2.behavior: Signals an error for the semantically empty delete_rrset operation against an absent RRset.
group_2.post_state: No change; n.campus.edu. retains only its A RRset and remains TXT-absent.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.2 and §3.4.2 define prerequisites and delete_rrset semantics, but do not mandate a single outcome when the name is already established by prerequisite and the targeted RRset is absent. Implementations may therefore either accept the update as a no-op or reject it with NXRRSET, yielding a reply-code divergence without a post-state difference.
